title: Exposing The Trillion Dollar Industry Built On Sickness (ft. Calley Means)

chunks.title: Introduction and Context
chunks.summary: The podcast begins with a discussion of the health crisis in America and how the healthcare industry profits from chronic diseases. The host sets the stage for a conversation about food policy and human health amidst a backdrop of governmental corruption in these sectors.
chunks.transcript: [Music] Pharmaceuticals make money when you can prescribe more pills for long periods of time and now there's 4.6 billion prescriptions written in America per year we have 10,000 chemicals in our food that are not allowed in other countries .5% of a child's brain today is microplastic we live 10 years less than the friends in Switzerland we're suffering we're suffering what is up guys welcome to

chunks.title: Discussion with C Means
chunks.summary: The host brings on C Means to delve deeper into the connections between governmental actions and food policies. They emphasize the importance of the Make America Healthy Again movement and the need for political solutions to health problems in the country.
chunks.transcript: this week's episode of my podcast yes I am wearing a pinstripe suit I thought I would do for you all because I love you so much I am at the America Fest conference in Phoenix and I got to sit down with my good buddy c means cie and I sat down about a year ago and talked about food policy talked about his history in lobbying groups or at least Consulting for these types of organizations he has a very unique perspective on what's going on at so many levels in terms of food policy and execution of way that our government is connected with food in this country he has been an in instumental central part of the Maha movement the make America healthy again movement so it was really a joy a pleasure and a privilege to sit down with him when I was in Phoenix Arizona to talk about what he's seen since the election whether you're on either side of the aisle this is not a political podcast I'm a doctor I'm interested in human health and I believe that the America healthy again movement is going to do many good things over the next four years I'm super excited about what RFK Jr has in store we talked about all of that and much more in this podcast with c means so please enjoy

chunks.title: Profitability of Chronic Disease
chunks.summary: C Means discusses how the healthcare system is structured to benefit from chronic diseases, citing economic statistics and how pharmaceutical companies thrive on long-term prescriptions. He reveals that insurance companies are incentivized to increase costs due to their profit structure.

chunks.summary: The podcast discusses the intersection between health and political agendas, emphasizing that health should not be a partisan issue. They reflect on how recent political movements have begun to prioritize health as a central issue.

chunks.title: Personal Responsibility and Societal Change
chunks.summary: They discuss the importance of personal responsibility in health but highlight that societal and systemic changes are crucial. They argue that the public needs to be educated about healthful eating and the impacts of current food policies.

chunks.title: Closing Thoughts on the Movement
chunks.summary: The podcast wraps up with encouraging remarks about the potential for the Make America Healthy Again movement to influence public health positively. The speakers acknowledge the importance of collective effort in driving change in health policy.
